text: Sealed into a one-person tunnel pod. It is exactly one person sized. The backpack disagrees.
Pod launched into the tunnel system. Acceleration: rude. Snacks: unsecured.
Hurtling through pitch-black tunnels. Something scraped the hull. The pod's speaker played a calming chime, badly.
The pod intercom crackled: 'Arrival in T-minus soon.' Reassuring. Specific.
Passed another pod going the opposite direction at speed. Brief eye contact through two layers of scratched plexiglass. A bond was formed.
Remembered the thing they forgot in Desperanza. The pod does not have a reverse gear. Noted for next time.
Did a pre-deployment stretch. Felt surprisingly athletic for three seconds.
Double-checked the deployment gear. Everything looked correct. Suspiciously correct.
Exchanged a confident nod with the team. Confidence was 40% real.
Tightened my boots. Felt ready for anything. Except robots. And running.
Took one last sip of synth-coffee. It tasted like bravery and battery acid.
Everyone synced their comms. No one admitted they forgot how to do it.
Put on gloves dramatically. No one saw it, but I felt cool.
Adjusted my gear straps. They immediately felt worse. I pretended they didn’t.
Someone cracked a joke before deployment. It was terrible. Morale improved anyway.
Cleaned my visor. Immediately smudged it again. Classic.
Roll call completed. Everyone answered with varying levels of enthusiasm.
Ate a quick snack before deploying. It was 80% crumbs.
Tapped my helmet twice for luck. Helmet responded with a hollow thunk.
Team struck a heroic pose for no reason. It helped.
Checked my pockets. Found a snack wrapper and a single bullet. Good enough.
Team huddle achieved. No strategy discussed. Still felt productive.
Had a brief moment of doubt. Ignored it successfully.
Performed a radio check. Heard my own voice echo back. Sounded heroic.
Gave myself a reassuring shoulder pat. It helped more than expected.
Team took a synchronized deep breath. Immediately coughed out of sync.
Heard a satisfying click while securing my gear. Confidence increased by 12%.
Checked my shoelaces. They were fine. I checked again anyway.
Team fist-bump executed flawlessly. Morale skyrocketed.
Took a sip from my canteen. It was mostly backwash. Still refreshing.
Heard something rattle in my pack. Decided it was future-me’s problem.
Someone gave a pep talk. It was incoherent but inspiring.
Helmet fogged up immediately. Took it as a sign of enthusiasm.
Ready check completed. Everyone lied confidently.
Did one more stretch. Pulled something. Ignored it.
Snapped my gloves dramatically. Felt like a professional.
Team lined up perfectly. Immediately ruined the formation by fidgeting.
Lifted my pack to gauge weight. It felt heavier than my hopes.
Someone saluted dramatically. No one knew why, but it felt right.
Reviewed the checklist. Skipped half of it. Felt efficient.
Shook my pack to make sure nothing was loose. Everything was loose.
Team took the first step toward the dropship in perfect sync. Immediately lost sync.
Tilted my helmet slightly to look cooler. It worked.
Team energy was high. Possibly too high. Someone was vibrating.
Took one last deep breath. It tasted like dust and determination.
Struck a ready pose. Immediately regretted how dramatic it was.
Final gear check complete. Everything was technically functional.
Someone started a team chant. It fell apart instantly but morale still rose.
Rolled my shoulders like a pro athlete. Felt like a pro athlete for 0.4 seconds.
Waved at another squad deploying. They waved back awkwardly.
Tried to secure a strap. Missed the buckle entirely.
